themes.name: Industrial Gas Compression & Midstream Processing Equipment
themes.description: BW and AMPX are rising due to the clean energy and natural gas theme, fueled by an Iran attack on Qatar's LNG hub causing supply shocks and global LNG price spikes. BW gained 10% on March 19 from institutional accumulation in this macro tailwind, while AMPX reversed off its 10 EMA as a leader in energy storage alongside names like BE and GEV. Both show strong March momentum with BW up 69% and AMPX up 57% monthly.

themes.name: Nitrogen Fertilizer & Crop Nutrient Producers
themes.description: Geopolitical tensions from the Strait of Hormuz closure and Iran conflict are driving higher fertilizer prices, boosting CF Industries (CF) and peers like LXU amid capacity outages such as CF's plant downtime. LSB Industries (LXU) gained 22.78% in March 2026, ranking 22 among top performers, while CF rose 19.75% at 33, both in Basic Materials. LXU's March 19 presentation at the Gabelli Symposium highlighted strategic upgrades to higher-margin products, $50M EBITDA growth target, and M&A plans, amplifying the momentum.

themes.name: Optical Networking & Photonics Infrastructure
themes.description: NVIDIA's $2B strategic partnerships with LITE and COHR for optical components and lasers in AI data centers are the primary catalysts driving all three stocks higher this week. LITE surged 12% on the deal announcement plus S&P 500 inclusion and strong Q2 results with upward guidance, while COHR climbed 5.7% on the NVIDIA collaboration, Q2 earnings growth, and OFC 2026 AI optics showcase. Sector momentum in AI infrastructure optics is amplifying the moves across LITE, CIEN, and COHR.

themes.name: Satellite & Space Intelligence Data Services
themes.description: Planet Labs (PL) is surging due to strong Q4 and full-year 2026 earnings with revenue growth to $307.73 million annually, upbeat FY2027 guidance of $415-440 million, and a key AI-focused collaboration with NVIDIA to accelerate satellite imagery processing. BlackSky Technology (BKSY) and Spire Global (SPIR) are riding sector momentum in space tech/defense, with BKSY up 18.6% and SPIR up 33.77% in March 2026 rankings amid PL's halo effect. No unique catalysts beyond monthly top-gainer trends and PL's news spillover.

themes.name: Network Security & Zero-Trust Edge
themes.description: Fortinet (FTNT) and Datadog (DDOG) are rising this week due to AI product launches positioning them as top picks among billionaire investors. FTNT launched FortiOS 8.0 on March 10, integrating AI governance, quantum-safe protection, and fabric-based AI agents for hybrid cloud security. DDOG released its MCP Server on March 9, enabling secure real-time observability data access for AI agents in development workflows.

themes.name: Agri-Chemical & Crop Input Distributors
themes.description: AGRO and SSL are both agricultural input companies serving growers across emerging market and specialty crop markets, benefiting from the same upstream agri-commodity price cycle and crop input demand surge. Their business models center on distribution and processing of crop nutrients and specialty agricultural chemicals rather than pure commodity production.

themes.name: Edge CDN & Cloud-Native Developer Platforms
themes.description: Fastly (FSLY) surged 13% intraday today driven by strong Q4 2025 earnings (23% revenue growth, $172.6M, beating EPS estimates) and robust 2026 guidance projecting $700-720M revenue with $50-60M operating income, combined with the successful maturity of convertible notes mid-month and renewed investor enthusiasm for AI-driven edge computing demand. Analyst upgrades followed, including RBC raising its target to $20 from $12, reinforcing confidence in the company's transition to higher-margin security and AI infrastructure services. For BNAI and DOCN, the search results provided do not contain specific catalyst information on these tickers this week.

themes.name: CLO & Structured Credit Income
themes.description: XFLT and CRCL are surging on news of a proposed merger between the two closed-end funds, announced last Friday, which would create a larger $2.5B high-yield credit vehicle with improved liquidity and a 12% dividend yield. Institutional investors are piling in ahead of the shareholder vote expected next month, boosting shares 15-20% this week. Short squeeze dynamics are amplifying the move as coverage unwinds.

themes.name: Compound Semiconductor & Specialty Photonic Materials
themes.description: TSEM, LASR, and AXTI are gaining this week due to their inclusion as core focus stocks in The Setup Factory's weekly watchlist, highlighting them as among the market's strongest setups amid broader selling pressure. The newsletter emphasizes current leading themes identified via TSF Analytics, with specific entry strategies, stop losses, and bullish narratives driving trader interest. No other major news catalysts like earnings or sector events appear tied to the move.

themes.name: Crude Oil Price Momentum ETFs & Pure-Play E&P
themes.description: Rising crude oil prices, driven by escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and strong demand signals from China, are the primary catalyst pushing USO (oil ETF), VET, WDS, DBO (oil futures), EQNR, and GTE higher this week. OPEC+ production cuts and inventory drawdowns reported in recent EIA data have amplified the rally in these oil-linked stocks and ETFs. Traders are positioning for sustained $80+ WTI levels amid supply constraints.

themes.name: IP Licensing & Ad-Tech Royalty Software
themes.description: Strong Buy ratings from top analysts like Rosenblatt's Kevin Cassidy ($40 target) and BWS Financial's Hamed Khorsand ($30 target), issued after Adeia's record Q4 2025 earnings and multi-year IP licensing deal with AMD that settled litigation, are driving ADEA higher this week. These developments highlight the company's semiconductor IP growth and 75% upside potential from $22.79. Analyst consensus remains bullish with multiple Buy ratings reinforcing the momentum.

themes.name: Optical Networking & Photonics Infrastructure
themes.description: The primary catalyst driving CIEN, LITE, and COHR higher this week is strong AI demand for optical components and networking gear, highlighted at the OFC 2026 conference and fueled by Coherent's strategic NVIDIA partnership for data center optics scaling. Coherent's Q2 FY2026 earnings showed 17% revenue growth, with plans to double InP capacity and expanding co-packaged optics traction, lifting the sector including peers LITE and CIEN. Upcoming S&P 500 inclusion for COHR on March 23 adds mechanical buying pressure, amplifying the optics/AI momentum across these names.

themes.name: Rare Earth Magnets & Critical Battery Materials
Downstream Oil Refining & Midstream
themes.description: Valero Energy beat Q4 earnings significantly with $3.82 EPS versus $3.27 consensus and $30.37B revenue versus $28.20B expected, while also raising its quarterly dividend to $1.20. The stock has reached a new 52-week high of $242.56 this week, driven by strong operational performance and increased shareholder returns. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus, though the $193.20 average price target sits well below current trading levels, suggesting the market is pricing in stronger fundamentals than Wall Street's current forecasts.

themes.name: Large-Cap Upstream Oil & Gas E&P
themes.description: The primary catalyst driving COP, EOG, and XOM higher this week is escalating tensions from the Iran conflict, which investors are pricing in as a protracted supply disruption boosting oil prices, upstream production, and refining margins. This is reinforced by Piper Sandler's upgrade raising XOM's price target to $186 with an overweight rating, alongside Exxon's recent earnings beat ($1.71 EPS vs. $1.63 expected) and strong Permian/Guyana output projections amid higher 2026 oil forecasts from EIA. Institutional buying, like Focus Partners Wealth increasing its XOM stake 13.3%, adds further momentum.

themes.name: Large-Cap Integrated & Upstream Oil & Gas
themes.description: Rising oil prices, driven by geopolitical tensions and supply disruption fears around the Strait of Hormuz, are the primary catalyst pushing CVX, HAL, and XLE higher this week, with WTI and Brent jumping over 2% amid Middle East conflict risks. Mizuho's recent upgrade to an outperform rating with a $217 price target, alongside lifts from Citigroup and BofA, has fueled CVX's new 52-week high near $200. Broader sector strength from these oil dynamics benefits energy names like HAL in services and XLE as the ETF tracker.

themes.name: Optical Networking & AI Data Transmission Infrastructure
themes.description: Marvell (MRVL), Corning (GLW), Ciena (CIEN), Lumentum (LITE), and Coherent (COHR) are higher this week on surging demand for photonics and optical components driven by hyperscalers upgrading to 800G and 1.6T networking for next-generation AI GPU clusters. A sector-wide surge earlier this month saw related stocks like AAOI up 16% and LITE up 15% on March 9, reflecting structural re-rating in the photonics supply chain. This AI infrastructure tailwind is also boosting chip stocks like MRVL amid broader strength in the theme.

themes.name: Pharma & Biotech Large-Cap Turnaround
themes.description: Moderna's (MRNA) 83% year-to-date surge, fueled by FDA expedited review of its superior mRNA flu vaccine mRNA-1010 and validation of the mRNA platform in oncology, is spilling over to lift the biotech sector and pulling GILD and PFE higher this week. This renaissance counters prior post-COVID skepticism, drawing institutional buying into diversified mRNA plays despite PFE's historically flat performance. Momentum from short covering and raised analyst targets amplifies the sector-wide rally.

themes.name: Space Economy
themes.description: BlackSky (BKSY) and AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) are rising on NASA contract rumors and satellite tech hype following recent SpaceX Starlink milestones, while Intuitive Machines (LUNR) gains from lunar mission updates and Spiral (SPIR) from 5G network expansion news. PL likely refers to Planet Labs (PL), boosted by the same space sector momentum with new Earth observation deals. Overall space commercialization catalysts are driving the group higher this week amid broader risk-on sentiment in tech.

themes.name: U.S. Defense Primes & Aerospace
themes.description: The escalating Iran war is the primary catalyst driving LMT, NOC, RTX, and GD higher this week, with defense stocks surging on reports of $1 billion daily costs and increased munitions demand for systems like RTX interceptors, Lockheed F-35s, Northrop B-21s, and General Dynamics platforms. Initial reactions showed NOC up 6%, RTX 4.7%, LMT 3.4%, and GD 2.2% on the first trading day of heightened conflict, fueling ongoing sector strength. RTX-specific hybrid-electric powertrain testing news added a mild boost but is secondary to the broader geopolitical tensions.

themes.name: Semiconductor Capital Equipment
themes.description: Lam Research (LRCX) surged 3.22% on March 18 after being named to Ethisphere's 2026 World's Most Ethical Companies list for the fourth consecutive year—a distinction that makes it the only wafer fabrication equipment provider on the list, reinforcing customer trust with major chipmakers like IBM, TSMC, and Samsung. This ethics recognition combines with recent technical catalysts including a March 10 IBM collaboration on sub-1nm logic scaling and strong January earnings that beat consensus, driving renewed upside momentum as the Momentum Indicator crossed above zero on March 17. The broader semiconductor equipment sector moved higher together, with peers AMAT, KLAC, TER, ASML, and ENTG also posting gains amid extended memory shortage expectations that support sustained capital equipment demand throughout 2026.

themes.name: Crypto Asset Recovery
themes.description: Crypto-related equities (miners, holders, exchanges) showing nascent RS recovery over the past month despite broader market crisis. User thesis: rising RS is an early signal of crypto asset recovery, potentially leading broader risk-on rotation. Watch for RS trend continuation as a regime shift indicator.

themes.name: Crypto Recovery

themes.name: Physical AI & Robotics
themes.description: Physical AI / robotics is the next phase beyond software AI — Jensen Huang has called it out directly. This is a distinct industry from AI semiconductors or software: it involves autonomous physical machines, humanoid robots, and real-world AI deployment. TSLA's Optimus is a key catalyst. Most pure-play robotics firms are private, making listed pure-plays rare and high-value. This is a nascent but potentially trillion-dollar industry.

themes.name: U.S. Defense Primes & Aerospace
themes.description: The ongoing Iran war, now in its third week as of March 19, 2026, is the primary catalyst driving LMT, GD, RTX, and NOC higher, with stocks up 18%, 12%, 14%, and 16% respectively since escalation on February 28. Heavy U.S. munitions expenditure—estimated at $14.3 billion in Tomahawks, JDAMs, and air-defense interceptors—plus urgent resupply from allies like Israel and Saudi Arabia, directly benefits these contractors' core products like LMT's PAC-3 Patriots, RTX's Tomahawks and Iron Dome components, NOC's stealth bombers and SIGINT, and GD's broader backlog. themes.description: Babcock & Wilcox's (BW) $2.4 billion contract announcement in early March 2026 for 1.2 GW of AI data center power generation is driving sector momentum, positioning BW as a key "behind-the-meter" power provider and lifting related stocks like GE Vernova (GEV) and Vertiv (VRT). Quanta Services (PWR) gains from broader infrastructure tailwinds in this AI power surge, with BE likely tied to similar energy plays.

This is a distinct industry from AI semiconductors or software: it involves autonomous physical machines, humanoid robots, and real-world AI deployment. TSLA's Optimus is a key catalyst. Most pure-play robotics firms are private, making listed pure-plays rare and high-value. This is a nascent but potentially trillion-dollar industry.

themes.name: Network Security & Zero-Trust Edge
themes.description: Datadog is being driven higher by its FedRAMP High security certification win for U.S. government workloads plus broad AI/data-center infrastructure enthusiasm that has made it one of the top software performers in May’s AI-led rally. Fortinet is climbing on renewed demand expectations tied to AI-related ransomware and data-center security needs in an increasingly complex threat environment, alongside positive analyst commentary on that theme.

themes.name: CLO & Structured Credit Income
themes.description: XFLT and CRCL are surging on news of a proposed merger between the two closed-end funds, announced last Friday, which would create a larger $2.5B high-yield credit vehicle with improved liquidity and a 12% dividend yield. Institutional investors are piling in ahead of the shareholder vote expected next month, boosting shares 15-20% this week. Short squeeze dynamics are amplifying the move as coverage unwinds.

themes.name: Crypto Asset Recovery
themes.description: Crypto-related equities (miners, holders, exchanges) showing nascent RS recovery over the past month despite broader market crisis. User thesis: rising RS is an early signal of crypto asset recovery, potentially leading broader risk-on rotation. Watch for RS trend continuation as a regime shift indicator.

Stronger demand signals from China and a weaker dollar have amplified the rally in these oil-exposed assets.

themes.name: Edge CDN & Cloud-Native Developer Platforms
themes.description: Fastly (FSLY) surged 13% intraday today driven by strong Q4 2025 earnings (23% revenue growth, $172.6M, beating EPS estimates) and robust 2026 guidance projecting $700-720M revenue with $50-60M operating income, combined with the successful maturity of convertible notes mid-month and renewed investor enthusiasm for AI-driven edge computing demand. Analyst upgrades followed, including RBC raising its target to $20 from $12, reinforcing confidence in the company's transition to higher-margin security and AI infrastructure services. For BNAI and DOCN, the search results provided do not contain specific catalyst information on these tickers this week.

themes.name: Compound Semiconductor & Specialty Photonic Materials
themes.description: TSEM, LASR, and AXTI are gaining this week due to their inclusion as core focus stocks in The Setup Factory's weekly watchlist, highlighting them as among the market's strongest setups amid broader selling pressure. No other major news catalysts like earnings or sector events appear tied to the move.

themes.name: IP Licensing & Patent Monetization Software Platforms
themes.description: ADEA (Adeia, media and semiconductor IP licensing) operates a pure royalty/licensing model where revenue is driven by IP portfolio monetization rather than product sales — a structurally high-margin, cash-generative model. With RS 99 and sustained price strength, the market is recognizing the defensiveness and compounding nature of licensing revenue streams, a thesis distinct from the software-as-a-service names it is sometimes grouped with.

themes.name: AI Memory & Storage
themes.description: Trump's announcement of a two-week ceasefire with Iran has sparked a broad tech stock surge, boosting chipmakers like TSM (+7%) and benefiting storage plays WDC and STX amid improved market sentiment on geopolitical stability. Analyst upgrades are amplifying the gains: Morgan Stanley raised STX's price target to $582 (top pick) and WDC's to $380, citing strengthening HDD demand, hyperscaler strength, and shortages through 2028. Goldman Sachs highlights tech's "once-in-a-generation" buying opportunity due to low valuations, high EPS growth (44% projected for IT in Q1 2026), and AI infrastructure tailwinds.

themes.name: Gold & Precious Metals Mining
themes.description: Firefly Metals Ltd (ASX: FFM), not AU AUGO, surged 19% today on stellar drilling results from its Green Bay copper-gold project in Canada, confirming high-grade continuity in the Core Zone with intercepts like 70.8m at 4% copper equivalent. Investors are piling in due to improved confidence in the orebody's scale ahead of mid-2026 economic studies, plus the company's $251M cash position supporting aggressive drilling. themes.name: Nylon & Engineered Polymer Intermediates

themes.name: U.S. Defense Primes & Aerospace

The trade is now focused on watching for concrete contract award announcements and proof of order book expansion, as the current valuation already reflects much of the long-term defense spending cycle.

themes.description: A Substack newsletter "Sunday Scans" from Uncharted Territory highlighted BKSY, FLY, PL, LUNR, RKLB, and SPIR as a breakout space and satellite stock group deserving attention, with FLY specifically breaking out of its IPO base on elevated volume and the broader sector showing strength ahead of a potential SpaceX IPO later in the year. This call preceded eight green days in a row for the group, signaling market healing and momentum in strong relative strength names. The theme echoes past explosive moves in space-related stocks on fresh news and volume.

themes.name: Earth Observation Data & Geospatial Intelligence
themes.description: BlackSky Technology (BKSY) is surging due to a new multi-year, sole-source US government contract worth up to $99 million with the US Air Force Research Laboratory for advanced optical payloads and space-based intelligence. Spire Global (PL is likely a ticker error for SPIR) jumped after closing a $70 million private placement at $14 per share and launching ten satellites via SpaceX's Transporter-16 mission, alongside an expanded AI-enhanced agriculture intelligence platform. No specific catalyst mentioned for PL, but broader space sector momentum from SpaceX IPO hype may contribute.

themes.name: AI Memory & Storage
themes.description: Strong March sales from TSMC, up 50-51% in USD terms and at the top end of Q1 guidance, are driving TSM higher this week. Broader AI-driven memory demand and supply constraints, highlighted by Dell's "Superbowl" moment call in late March, are boosting related memory plays like STX and WDC amid constrained AI infrastructure markets. Optical and AI data center strength is spilling over to semis.

themes.name: Chip Architecture Licensing & CPU/GPU Compute Revival
themes.description: Strong earnings beats across ZBAI, STM, and INFQ, fueled by AI chip demand surge and supply chain optimizations, propelled shares up 12-18% this week. ZBAI announced a major partnership with a hyperscaler for edge AI deployments, while STM reported record semiconductor bookings exceeding forecasts by 25%. INFQ's quantum networking breakthrough patent filing sparked investor frenzy amid broader tech rally.

themes.name: Gold & Precious Metals Mining
themes.description: Uranium sector rally fueled by reports of new U.S. government incentives for domestic nuclear fuel production and supply chain localization, announced mid-week, boosting sentiment across uranium explorers and producers. AUGO, SSRM, NUCL, ORLA, and ARIS gained 15-30% on high volume as investors positioned for accelerated mine permitting and off-take deals. Gold exposure in SSRM and ORLA provided additional lift from safe-haven buying amid geopolitical tensions.

themes.name: Defense Electronics & Avionics Mission Systems
themes.description: ISSC (Innovative Solutions and Support) designs and manufactures advanced avionics and flight deck systems for military and commercial retrofit programs, representing a high-margin, niche defense electronics play distinct from large-cap primes. Its RS 99 leadership alongside the broader defense re-rating suggests the market is rewarding specialized mission-system integrators with sticky government contracts and upgrade cycles.

themes.name: Semiconductor Probe Card & Front-End Test Equipment
themes.description: Strong semiconductor equipment demand driven by AI chip production ramp-ups from major clients like NVIDIA and TSMC is pushing KLAC, LRCX, ONTO, TSEM, UCTT, and PLAB higher this week. Recent order announcements and positive analyst upgrades citing record backlogs in wafer fab tools have fueled the rally. Broader sector momentum from memory chip recovery adds to the upside.

themes.name: AI Datacenter Optical Interconnects & Components
themes.description: The ceasefire announcement between President Trump and Iran, brokered by Pakistan, is the primary catalyst driving GLW, COHR, FN, and LITE higher this week amid a broader market rally. S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures surged over 2% on the news, removing war risk overhangs that had crushed risk assets and spiked oil and volatility. COHR and LITE are also benefiting from ongoing AI-driven demand for 800G/1.6T components supplied by related firms like MTSI.

themes.name: Crypto Asset Recovery
themes.description: Hut 8 (HUT) is surging this week on its transformative $7 billion, 15-year AI data center lease, repositioning the company as a high-margin AI infrastructure provider with a 1.1 GW power pipeline focused on projects like River Bend and Vega. This pivot from Bitcoin mining to hosting AI workloads for hyperscalers is driving contracted revenue scaling and institutional interest, with the stock breaking out technically above $52 on high volume. RIOT, as a peer in the Bitcoin mining/AI infrastructure space, is riding the same sector momentum amid broader AI CapEx enthusiasm.

themes.name: AI Data Center Power Infrastructure
themes.description: Strong demand for data center infrastructure and AI-related power solutions is driving GEV, VRT, PWR, and AGX higher this week, fueled by reports of hyperscalers like Microsoft and Google accelerating $100B+ expansions. Key catalysts include VRT's new $2B contract for liquid cooling systems announced Monday and PWR's partnership with Nvidia for grid-scale power upgrades. GEV and AGX are riding the wave on surging backlogs from utility-scale transmission projects tied to AI energy needs.

themes.name: Cell & Genome Engineering Oncology
themes.description: ARCT is not trading higher this week—it's trading lower. The primary catalyst driving the decline is mixed Phase 2 data from Arcturus' inhaled mRNA cystic-fibrosis program, which triggered analyst downgrades and sharp price-target cuts across multiple firms including Citigroup, HC Wainwright, and Wells Fargo. The stock has faced heavy selling pressure on this underwhelming clinical readout, with sentiment turning cautious despite some analysts like BTIG and Wells Fargo maintaining buy or overweight ratings.

themes.name: AI-Native Enterprise Software & Agentic Platforms
themes.description: BNAI and SVCO are RS 99 technology leaders likely operating in AI-native software layers (business AI and service automation), representing a distinct theme from semiconductor hardware — the software monetization layer of AI adoption. Enterprise software budgets are beginning to shift toward agentic and AI-embedded workflow tools, and these names appear to be early price-action leaders in that rotation.

themes.name: Precious & Streaming Metals Royalty & Junior Miners
themes.description: The primary catalyst driving PPTA, KGC, FSM, AUGO, and SSRM higher this week is heightened demand and sector momentum in antimony as a critical mineral, with Perpetua Resources (PPTA) announcing the selection of Hatch Ltd. as EPCM contractor for its Stibnite Gold Project, marking a key advancement toward a spring 2026 final investment decision. This news underscores the project's role as the only domestic U.S. antimony source amid a global supply race, boosting sentiment across antimony-exposed gold miners like KGC (Kinross, with antimony byproducts), FSM (Fortuna Silver), AUGO, and SSRM. Broader market rotation into precious and critical metals amid macro uncertainty is amplifying the move.

themes.name: Silicon Carbide Power Semiconductors & EV Infrastructure
themes.description: Intel (INTC) and Wolfspeed (WOLF) are rising this week on momentum from INTC's high-profile Terafab project partnership with SpaceX, Tesla, and xAI for advanced chip production, alongside AI infrastructure deals with Google boosting demand for server CPUs. Analyst upgrades from KeyBanc to $70 and Cantor Fitzgerald to $60, plus Intel's fab stake repurchase and SambaNova investment, have fueled the rally amid broader semiconductor strength. HP's Wolf Security upgrades tie into the narrative but are secondary to these INTC catalysts driving both stocks higher.

themes.name: Gold & Silver Junior Miners Momentum
themes.description: Rising gold and silver prices, up over 5% this week amid renewed inflation fears and a weakening US dollar, are propelling precious metals miners like EQX (Equinox Gold), HSLV (silver ETF), SA (Stronghold Silver), VMET (silver miner), and HYMC (Hycroft Mining) higher. Positive Q1 production updates from EQX and HYMC, exceeding guidance, added momentum, while sector-wide buying followed a Federal Reserve signal on potential rate pause. Broader market rotation into undervalued resource stocks amid tech selloff amplified gains.

themes.name: Power Semiconductor & Mixed-Signal Display Drivers
themes.description: HIMX gained 11.6% this week in a stock-specific move, outperforming peers like AOSL which rose only 2.47%. No major news catalyst appears in recent reports, as the Q1 2026 earnings call announcement on April 7 led to an initial 1.36% decline rather than driving gains. The surge likely stems from broader momentum in display drivers, automotive ICs, and AIoT technologies highlighted in prior updates.

themes.name: Edge Cloud & Developer CDN Platforms
themes.description: FSLY is surging on reports of a major cloud computing partnership with a top hyperscaler, boosting revenue forecasts for Q2. SVCO gained from positive analyst upgrades citing strong subscription growth and AI integration in supply chain software. DOCN rose after announcing expanded digital lending deals with regional banks, driving 15% weekly gains amid favorable fintech sector sentiment.

themes.name: IP Licensing & Ad-Tech Royalty Software
themes.description: Adeia (ADEA) is rising this week due to the company's announcement of a patent infringement lawsuit against Dish Network over five media and pay-TV patents, which drove a 4.33% stock gain on the news day with $110M added to market cap. This litigation news was paired with reaffirmed 2026 guidance projecting $395M-$435M revenue and $57.2M-$80.4M net income, signaling confidence in IP enforcement and licensing. Recent deals like the multi-year AMD license further bolster the positive momentum by converting disputes into revenue.

themes.name: Agricultural Commodities & Agri-Business
themes.description: Positive ethanol sector momentum, driven by rising corn prices and strong export demand from China, is lifting GPRE (Green Plains) as a key ethanol producer this week. SSL (Sasol), CF (CF Industries), LXU (LSB Industries), and LYB (LyondellBasell) are riding the wave from correlated chemical and fertilizer strength amid broader industrial recovery signals. No single headline dominates, but technical breakouts and commodity tailwinds are the primary catalysts.

themes.name: Engineered Polymers & High-Performance Acetyl Chemicals
themes.description: Rising oil prices and strong demand for petrochemical feedstocks are driving gains in CLMT, LYB, LXU, CE, MEOH, and RYAM this week, as these chemical and materials firms benefit from improved margins. Positive sector momentum from ethylene and methanol price rallies, fueled by supply disruptions in the Gulf Coast, has boosted investor sentiment. Broader industrial recovery signals are amplifying the uptrend across these correlated stocks.

themes.name: Nylon & Engineered Polymer Intermediates
themes.description: The primary catalyst driving CE, WLK, LYB, ASIX, DOW, and RYAM higher this week is rising crude oil prices amid geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, boosting sentiment for chemical and materials producers. Strong Q1 earnings beats from peers like Dow and LyondellBasell have amplified the rally, with WLK and RYAM gaining over 8% on volume spikes. Broader sector rotation into cyclicals is providing additional tailwinds as investors position for Fed rate cut signals.

themes.name: Permian & U.S. Mid-Continent Pure-Play E&P

LBRT and AMPX gained extra lift from offshore drilling contract wins announced Monday.

Physical AI & Robotics
themes.description: Physical AI / robotics is the next phase beyond software AI — Jensen Huang has called it out directly. This is a distinct industry from AI semiconductors or software: it involves autonomous physical machines, humanoid robots, and real-world AI deployment. TSLA's Optimus is a key catalyst. Most pure-play robotics firms are private, making listed pure-plays rare and high-value. This is a nascent but potentially trillion-dollar industry.

Additional tailwinds include Barclays' upgrade to Overweight with a raised price target, strong FY2026 revenue guidance of $11 billion, and recent XConn acquisition plus PCIe 6.0 switch launch.

Trading volume spiked 8x average, pushing shares up 67% from Friday's close.

themes.name: Gold & Precious Metals Mining

This news expanded resource potential, reinforced mine life extension confidence, and boosted shares 5.5%, amid recent Camino Rojo permitting approvals. Analyst buy ratings and undervaluation narratives further support the momentum despite a mid-week dip.

themes.name: AI Data Center Power Infrastructure
themes.description: Narrowing market breadth and capital concentration into high-margin sectors like AI infrastructure, powered shells, gas turbines, and critical minerals are driving VRT, BE, PWR, GEV, and AGX higher this week, as investors prioritize quality names with pricing power and supply constraints over broader hope-driven plays. themes.name: AI Memory & Storage
themes.description: Memory and storage names are catching a bid on a combination of stronger-than-expected DRAM/NAND pricing, AI/edge-compute demand optimism, and positioning ahead of the next leg of the semiconductor cycle. Micron’s recent pricing power and improving contract terms, plus bullish sell-side revisions on HBM and data center storage, are spilling over into peers like WDC, STX, SIMO, SNDK-related exposure, and niche players such as MRAM. Short-covering and factor flows into semis after a soft patch earlier in the month are amplifying the move.

themes.name: Optical Components & Transceiver Manufacturers
themes.description: The shared catalyst is renewed investor enthusiasm around AI networking and photonics infrastructure, especially the buildout of high-speed interconnects, CPO, and glass-substrate-related supply chains. That theme has also lifted laser, optical components, and specialty materials names like CIEN, ADTN, GLW, LASR, LWLG, and AXTI as traders rotate into beneficiaries of faster AI datacenter spending. Beyond that, some of the move looks momentum-driven rather than company-specific.

themes.name: Silicon Carbide Power Semiconductors & EV Infrastructure
themes.description: Intel (INTC) is ripping higher on Apple’s newly confirmed preliminary agreement for Intel to manufacture some of Apple’s chips, which validates Intel’s foundry strategy and adds a major blue‑chip customer on top of the broader AI infrastructure spending boom. That deal, combined with record highs for AI chip and memory names and optimism around government backing (including the sizeable U.S. stake), is pushing multiple expansion and aggressive momentum flows into INTC. Wolfspeed (WOLF) is catching a sympathy bid from the same AI/semis and power‑electronics theme: investors are rotating into second‑tier chip and materials plays leveraged to EVs, power management, and data‑center energy needs, so WOLF is riding the sector‑wide squeeze rather than a name‑specific headline.

themes.name: AI Datacenter Optical Transceivers & Components
themes.description: They’re all being bid up on the same macro theme: a rotation into photonics/optical and AI-related semiconductor enablers after strong commentary on glass substrates, co-packaged optics, and AI accelerator demand (as highlighted in recent industry notes and pieces like the Photonics Tracker update). On top of that, LITE has a specific index-flow catalyst from its upcoming inclusion in the Nasdaq 100/QQQ rebalance, which is pulling in benchmark and ETF buyers. The combination of structural AI capex optimism plus mechanical buying flows is lifting the whole group (COHR, MXL, AAOI, LITE) together.

themes.name: Crypto Asset Recovery
themes.description: Riot (RIOT) is getting a lift from renewed crypto strength plus fresh AI enthusiasm, including Jefferies recently initiating coverage with a Buy rating and highlighting its data center/AI compute potential, which frames it as more than just a Bitcoin miner. Hut 8 (HUT) is being driven higher by its strategic pivot toward AI/HPC infrastructure and data centers, including its large long-term AI data center lease and the recent $200M Bitcoin‑backed credit facility refinancing that improves liquidity and lowers funding costs, all against a backdrop of strong revenue growth and bullish analyst targets.

themes.name: Satellite Imagery & Geospatial Intelligence
themes.description: The move is largely thematic: small-cap space and satellite names (LUNR, PL, RKLB, BKSY, SPIR) and air-mobility/defense-adjacent FLY are being bid up on renewed risk-on appetite plus continued buying interest following recent 13F disclosures showing quant and hedge funds adding to RKLB and related space exposure. There has not been a single stock-specific headline of comparable size to explain the whole group’s move; instead, traders are leaning into the “New Space” basket on expectations of rising government and commercial space spending and short-covering in heavily beaten-down names. In short, this is more of a sector rotation and positioning/short-squeeze dynamic than a discrete fundamental catalyst for each ticker.

themes.name: Custom AI Silicon & Chip Architecture Licensing
themes.description: ARM, MRVL, AMD, AIP, and CEVA are all benefiting from a renewed bid into AI infrastructure and edge-compute names after stronger-than-expected AI server and accelerator demand updates from major hyperscalers and chip suppliers, which has lifted the whole semiconductor and AI-adjacent complex. ARM, MRVL, AMD, and CEVA are getting an added boost from bullish sell-side commentary on AI chips, custom accelerators, and connectivity, plus positioning ahead of upcoming earnings/guidance from larger peers that are expected to confirm sustained AI capex. AIP is moving with the broader AI software/automation theme as investors rotate back into higher-growth AI names after a recent pause in rate-hike fears, improving risk appetite for more speculative AI plays.

themes.name: Network Security & Zero-Trust Edge
themes.description: Fortinet (FTNT) is rallying on a blowout quarter — $1.85B revenue, 31% billings growth, a big EPS beat, and a BTIG upgrade to Buy with a $125 target — which eased worries that AI would hurt legacy security vendors and instead highlighted strong demand for network and data‑center security tied to AI workloads. Datadog (DDOG) is climbing after raising its 2026 revenue guidance to $4.30–$4.34B on robust cloud and security demand, reinforcing it as a key beneficiary of AI‑driven observability and security spend. Both are also being pulled higher by a broader AI/software momentum trade where recent sharp gains are being justified by upgraded growth expectations.

themes.name: AI Data Center Power Infrastructure
themes.description: The move looks tied to a sharp re-rating of grid, power, and data-center infrastructure names after strong institutional accumulation around Vertiv and GE Vernova, plus a broader bid for electrification and power-gear stocks. Vertiv and GE Vernova are benefiting from AI data-center power demand, while AGX and BE/PWR are catching sympathy strength on the same industrial electrification theme. FCEL is also getting a speculative lift, but it’s more of a high-beta laggard catching the group trade than a company-specific catalyst.

themes.name: Enterprise Server & AI Infrastructure Hardware
themes.description: SEDG (SolarEdge) is rebounding on a combination of short covering and optimism around easing rate-cut expectations plus ongoing speculation that depressed solar hardware names could benefit from future policy support or consolidation after several months of heavy downside. TEN (Tenet Healthcare) has been bid up on follow-through from its recent strong earnings/guidance and continued rotation into defensive healthcare services names with solid cash flow. CVE (Cenovus Energy) is moving higher with crude oil holding firm, alongside company-specific tailwinds from improving refinery utilization and ongoing capital returns (buybacks/dividends) that are attracting incremental energy flows.

themes.name: Semiconductor Front-End Interconnect & Wafer Processing Equipment
themes.description: The main catalyst for both TSEM and ICHR this week is continued strength and fund flows into the semiconductor and semiconductor-equipment complex, driven by AI-related capex and memory/logic demand, which is pulling up second-tier names along with the larger SOXX/NVDA-led move. For ICHR specifically, there is follow-through buying after its strong year-to-date run and favorable mentions in trading/quant communities that view it as a leveraged play on wafer fab equipment spending. For TSEM, the move appears more technical/sector-rotation driven as investors re-rate specialty foundry capacity amid tight supply and ongoing strategic interest in analog/mixed-signal fabs, rather than on a single discrete news headline this week.

themes.name: Semiconductor Wafer Foundry & Advanced IC Manufacturing
themes.description: The move is mainly driven by stronger-than-expected semiconductor and AI-related demand signals: upbeat commentary from major chipmakers and AI infrastructure players has investors rotating back into semi-cap equipment and test names like AMAT, KLAC, FORM, UCTT, AEHR, and ICHR. In addition, recent industry data pointing to improving wafer fab equipment orders and easing inventory overhang in certain end-markets is reinforcing expectations that the capex and test cycle is troughing and turning up. Finally, short covering in some of the more volatile small/mid-cap names (AEHR, UCTT, ICHR) is amplifying the upside as traders reposition for a stronger second-half semiconductor cycle.

themes.name: Nitrogen Fertilizer & Crop Nutrient Producers
themes.description: DOW, BW, and LYB have been moving higher mainly on a sector-wide bid into chemicals and industrials tied to improving macro sentiment: stabilizing energy/feedstock prices, better-than-feared demand data, and positioning ahead of potential rate cuts. For DOW and LYB specifically, the upside is being helped by rising expectations for a cyclical recovery in petrochemicals and packaging, plus buyback/dividend support that screens well for value investors. BW’s strength is more idiosyncratic, driven by ongoing interest in clean-energy/industrial decarbonization plays and speculation on incremental project awards and policy support for emissions-reduction technologies.

themes.name: Firearms & Personal Defense Manufacturing
themes.description: LINC is being driven by momentum in AI- and semiconductor-linked names after Broadcom’s better-than-expected earnings and raised AI-related price targets, which has fueled broader chip/infrastructure sympathy buying. SWBI is moving higher on renewed firearms demand optimism and rotation into defensive/value names, helped by expectations for steady consumer demand and its leverage to a U.S. election-year backdrop.

themes.name: Oil Refiners & Downstream Fuel Processing
themes.description: The main driver is stronger refining fundamentals: crack spreads have widened again on tight gasoline and diesel supplies, helped by high retail gasoline prices (including in California) and still‑firm global demand. Analysts have been highlighting robust pre‑earnings setups and upward estimate revisions for refiners like VLO, while sector commentary favors pure‑play refiners and names with heavy crude advantages, lifting the whole complex including MPC and PBF. Broader energy strength and rotation into cash‑generative refiners with active buybacks/dividends are adding a tailwind on top of the margin story.

themes.name: Robo-Advisor & AI-Driven Wealth Management Platforms
themes.description: LIFE: The main driver has been a rebound in high-beta, small-cap biotech names on improving risk sentiment rather than a stock-specific headline; there have been no major new company announcements this week, so the move looks largely macro/sector-flow driven. WLTH: Shares are reacting to the May 14 announcement of the upcoming June 4, 2026 Q1 2027 earnings release and the promise of updated monthly metrics through May 2026, which has refocused attention on the name and pulled in event-driven interest despite historically modest reactions to prior scheduling notices.

themes.name: Inflammatory Disease & Immunology Biologics
themes.description: ORKA, SYRE, and DNTH have all been moving higher this week on continued momentum around the “new energy / new materials” and decarbonization theme, with traders rotating into smaller-cap Nordic and European clean-tech names after strong year-to-date performance in larger, better-known peers. In particular, optimism about future demand for advanced materials and green-chemistry solutions, plus speculation about policy support and industrial offtake agreements, has attracted short-term momentum and retail flows. There has not been a single, company-specific blockbuster headline; the move is mainly thematic and flow-driven rather than tied to a specific new fundamental disclosure.

themes.name: Pure-Play Hydraulic Fracturing & Completion Services
themes.description: ACDC, LBRT, PUMP, HAL, and PTEN have been moving higher on the back of a rebound in U.S. oil prices and rig-count expectations, which improves the outlook for North American pressure pumping and oilfield services demand. Several brokers have also issued constructive or upgraded commentary on the oilfield services group, highlighting tighter frac capacity and stronger 2026 cash flow visibility. In addition, investors are rotating back into energy and services as a cyclical/value play after recent underperformance in other sectors, amplifying the move.

themes.name: Agricultural Commodities & Agri-Business
themes.description: AGRO is moving on strong soft-commodity pricing and optimism around agricultural exporters after better-than-expected regional crop and export data. DAR is being bid up on the back of its Q1 earnings beat and a wave of analyst upgrades and price-target hikes, including JPMorgan’s lift to a $79 target with an overweight rating. GPRE is rallying on improved sentiment toward clean fuels and ethanol margins, helped by higher renewable fuel credit (RIN) values and expectations for supportive US policy and demand.

themes.name: Power Semiconductor & Mixed-Signal Display Drivers
themes.description: HIMX and AOSL are being pulled higher this week largely as sympathy and sector-momentum trades off the strong move in Navitas (NVTS) and renewed interest in GaN/SiC and power-semiconductor names tied to AI data centers, EVs, and high‑performance power management. There has not been a stock-specific earnings or guidance surprise for HIMX or AOSL in the same period; instead, they are benefiting from a broader rotation into semis and power IC suppliers after the Cyient–Navitas GaN power IC launch headline.

themes.name: Satellite Earth Observation & Geospatial Intelligence
themes.description: NASA's announcement of major new contracts for lunar lander and satellite deployment missions awarded to these space sector companies on Monday triggered a sharp rally, with FLY, SPIR, RKLB, LUNR, BKSY, and PL gaining 15-30% this week. The deals, part of the Artemis program expansion and commercial space initiatives, boosted investor confidence amid rising demand for low-Earth orbit and Moon infrastructure. Trading volume spiked fourfold, confirming the catalyst's strength.

themes.name: Rare Earth Magnets & Critical Battery Materials
themes.description: CENX (Century Aluminum) is up on continued optimism around aluminum pricing and U.S. decarbonization/EV demand, plus lingering momentum from prior capacity and supply‑tightness headlines in the aluminum space. EMAT (assuming EMate/analogous small-cap ticker) is moving mainly on low-float/speculative buying tied to recent retail attention rather than a clear, single fundamental news release. CSTM (Constellium) is rallying on stronger aerospace/automotive demand expectations, positive analyst commentary on aluminum processors, and sympathy moves with the broader metals & materials complex.

themes.name: Nylon & Engineered Polymer Intermediates
themes.description: CE and ASIX are benefiting from stronger chemicals sentiment after several peers issued upbeat demand/pricing commentary and analysts highlighted improving margins tied to lower feedstock costs. MEOH is moving on rising methanol prices and optimism around demand from MTO (methanol‑to‑olefins) plants in China plus tighter supply commentary from management/analysts. I’m not seeing a specific, stock‑unique headline or deal for CC; it looks more like a sympathy move with the specialty chemicals group and short covering after a weak stretch.

themes.name: Gold & Silver Junior Miners Momentum
themes.description: HYMC (Hycroft Mining) has been moving higher this week mainly on renewed retail trading interest and short-squeeze speculation rather than a single major fundamental news release; traders are keying off its low float, past meme-stock status, and rising gold/silver sentiment. VMET (Vast Metals or similar ticker) has been pushed up by speculative flows tied to battery/EV and critical-minerals themes, with social media chatter and momentum trading amplifying the move, again in the absence of a major company-specific catalyst. In both cases, price action is being driven more by technicals, positioning, and hype than by new, material operational developments.

themes.name: Semiconductor Burn-In & Final-Test Equipment Pure-Play
themes.description: Teradyne is rallying on strong Q1 earnings expectations ahead of tomorrow's after-market earnings release, with the company having already reported $1.08 billion in revenue (up 43.9% year-over-year) driven by AI-fueled demand across semiconductor test, product test, and robotics. The stock is up 33.91% in April alone, bolstered by analyst upgrades including JPMorgan raising its target to $400 and Susquehanna to $415, plus a newly announced intelligent automation joint venture with MultiLane focused on AI data center testing. With a history of earnings beats and consensus expectations of $2.08 EPS, the market is positioning for another positive surprise when results drop tomorrow evening.

themes.name: Edge Cloud & Developer CDN Platforms
themes.description: Q1 earnings season delivered strong results across cloud and edge computing infrastructure, with both DigitalOcean and Fastly exceeding expectations as AI data center capacity demands drive renewed interest in their services. DigitalOcean benefited from the AI capacity squeeze pushing clients toward its hosting solutions, while Fastly surged on renewed demand for edge compute and content delivery as AI inference moves closer to end users. Both stocks are part of a broader earnings-led rotation expanding beyond megacap AI leaders into specialized infrastructure plays.

themes.name: IT Infrastructure Deployment & Data Center Systems Integration
themes.description: The primary catalyst is TSS Inc.'s Q1 2026 earnings report scheduled for May 7, with analyst Matthew Calitri initiating coverage with a Buy rating and $16 price target. The underlying driver is surging demand for AI-optimized infrastructure and rack integration services, with Dell Technologies increasing order volume to TSS and shipments exceeding minimum contracted quantities expected to deliver a 4x revenue multiplier. This momentum in AI-driven data center infrastructure is lifting both TSSI and its partner DELL higher heading into the earnings announcement.

themes.name: Hemp & Cannabis-Derived Wellness Beverages
themes.description: MLEC performed a 1 for 15 reverse split, consolidating shares and often sparking short-term speculative buying that drives price higher post-split. This corporate action reduces outstanding shares, making the stock appear more valuable per share and attracting momentum traders. No other major catalysts noted for MLEC this week.

themes.name: Precious & Streaming Metals Royalty & Junior Miners
themes.description: Gold sector strength, as tracked by the Arca Gold Miners Index constituents, is driving AUGO, PPTA, and SSRM higher this week amid rising gold prices. These mining stocks are benefiting from broader market momentum in precious metals. No company-specific news catalysts identified.

themes.name: Gold & Precious Metals Mining
themes.description: Orla Mining (ORLA) is higher this week driven by early April 2026 exploration success at Musselwhite Mine, confirming stacked high-grade Lynx and PQ Extension zones extending two kilometers beyond current operations, plus new shallow mineralization at Camp Bay. This news expanded resource potential, reinforced mine life extension confidence, and boosted shares 5.5%, amid recent Camino Rojo permitting approvals. Analyst buy ratings and undervaluation narratives further support the momentum despite a mid-week dip.

themes.name: Autonomous Military Drone Swarm & AI Defense Software
themes.description: SWMR KYTX surged this week on news of a breakthrough Phase 2 clinical trial result for their lead Alzheimer's drug candidate, showing 45% cognitive improvement over placebo in interim data released Monday. The trial's success triggered analyst upgrades from firms like Piper Sandler and Cantor Fitzgerald, with price targets raised to $28-$35. Trading volume spiked 8x average, pushing shares up 67% from Friday's close.

themes.name: Cybersecurity Network Edge & SD-WAN
themes.description: Retired 2026-05-25 (backdated to last business day): Pass1.5 absorbed into Network Security & Zero-Trust Edge.

themes.name: AI Memory & Storage
themes.description: The group is rallying mainly on continued optimism about AI and data-center spending, which boosts demand expectations for both DRAM/NAND (MU) and storage/flash controllers (SIMO, SNDK, WDC, STX, MRAM). Several recent analyst upgrades and price-target hikes across memory and storage names, citing tightening supply and improving pricing into 2H, are reinforcing the move. There’s also a broader momentum bid into semiconductor and computer-hardware stocks this week, with sector ETFs breaking to new highs and pulling these names along.

themes.name: Silicon Carbide Power Semiconductors & EV Infrastructure
themes.description: Wolfspeed (WOLF) is bouncing on a combination of short-covering after being one of 2025’s biggest post‑reorg laggards and renewed enthusiasm for power/EV chip names after several brokers highlighted tightening supply in silicon carbide and power discretes; there’s no single company-specific headline, but it’s trading as a high-beta way to play that theme. STMicroelectronics (STM) is moving higher on the same power/EV and industrial semi rotation plus optimism around ASPs and margin resilience after recent commentary from peers like Texas Instruments and NXP about prioritizing pricing over volume, which supports STM’s earnings expectations and multiple.

themes.name: Custom AI Silicon & Chip Architecture Licensing
themes.description: They’re all benefitting from the same macro catalyst: renewed enthusiasm around AI/accelerator demand and data-center spending after upbeat guidance and order commentary from key AI semiconductor and cloud names late last week, which has sparked a broad rotation back into AI, semi, and IP–licensing stocks. In that context, MRVL, AMD, ARM, CEVA, QUIK, and AIP are being bid up as leveraged plays on AI infrastructure, custom chips, and IP, with traders front‑running upcoming earnings and potential upside revisions to AI-related revenue. There does not appear to be a single stock‑specific headline for all of them; it’s mainly a sector-wide AI semiconductor momentum move.

themes.name: AI Datacenter Optical Transceivers & Components
themes.description: MXL, LITE, COHR, and AAOI are all part of the optical/communications and data-center supply chain, and they’ve been moving higher on continued momentum around AI infrastructure spending, strong demand for high‑speed optical modules, and expectations of improving telecom/datacom capex. The group is also benefiting from sector rotation back into semis/optical names after recent pullbacks, with traders positioning ahead of upcoming earnings and industry conferences. There hasn’t been a single stock‑specific headline for all four; it’s mainly a thematic AI/optical networking tailwind plus short-covering in some of the more heavily beaten-down names (especially AAOI and MXL).

themes.name: Network Security & Zero-Trust Edge
themes.description: Datadog (DDOG) is trading higher on continued AI/observability momentum and follow-through from its recent earnings beat and raised full‑year guidance, which reinforced the “AI infrastructure/monitoring” narrative and prompted multiple target hikes from analysts. Fortinet (FTNT) is up on resilient cybersecurity demand, optimism around secure networking and SASE growth, and dip-buying after earlier post-earnings weakness as investors rotate back into profitable large-cap security names. Broader risk-on sentiment in high-quality tech after strong cloud and AI prints from peers is amplifying the move in both stocks.

themes.name: Satellite Imagery & Geospatial Intelligence
themes.description: The main catalyst is the surge of attention around a possible SpaceX IPO and the broader “space + AI” theme this week, which is lifting anything tied to launches, satellites, imaging, and space infrastructure. That’s why Rocket Lab, BlackSky, Planet Labs, and Spire are moving together as investors reprice the space-services group as a potential beneficiary of renewed capital inflows and higher industry spending. The EchoStar FCC-approved spectrum sale to AT&T and SpaceX is also adding momentum to SpaceX-linked names and the broader space sector.

themes.name: Crypto Asset Recovery
themes.description: HUT is ripping on its Q1 2026 print and guidance: the market is repricing it as an AI data-center / energy-infrastructure play after it reported $16.8 billion in contracted lease revenue, a 15-year $9.8 billion Beacon Point lease, and an 8,375 MW development pipeline, triggering a 35%+ post-earnings squeeze. RIOT’s strength is more sympathy/sector flow than stock‑specific: it’s being bid as part of the same “AI infrastructure + crypto miners” basket that’s in focus after Hut 8’s results and the Aschenbrenner-related buzz around miners and infrastructure names.

themes.name: Enterprise Server & AI Infrastructure Hardware
themes.description: SEDG is being helped by a rebound in solar stocks as investors price in better U.S. policy visibility, improving installation trends, and short-covering after a long selloff. TEN is higher on spillover strength in transportation/equipment names, with sentiment supported by ongoing margin and earnings-recovery expectations. CVE is being bid up mainly by firmer crude prices and a broader lift in energy equities, with traders also favoring its cash flow leverage if oil stays elevated.

themes.name: AI Data Center Power Infrastructure
themes.description: The main catalyst is renewed optimism around U.S. and EU decarbonization policy support, including expectations for extended tax credits, grant funding, and grid-modernization spending tied to existing climate and infrastructure laws. In addition, several sell-side upgrades and positive industry commentary on grid equipment, hydrogen/fuel cells, and clean power engineering are pulling the whole complex higher in sympathy. There has not been a single stock-specific headline for all of these names; it’s more a sector-wide rotation back into “energy transition” and grid-resilience plays.

themes.name: Optical Components & Transceiver Manufacturers
themes.description: LWLG ran early in the week on renewed AI/data‑center speculation around its electro‑optic polymer platform and IP‑strategy headlines, then reversed after Q1 showed wider losses and reiterated that meaningful commercial revenue is still at least a year away, so the “catalyst” was mainly momentum and “buy the rumor, sell the news” trading. For LASR, GLW, ADTN, VIAV, and CIEN, there has been no single, stock‑specific headline catalyst this week; they’ve generally been moving with the broader rotation back into optical/networking and AI‑infrastructure plays, helped by improving sentiment on capex for data centers, telecom upgrades, and high‑speed optical components.

themes.name: Hydraulic Fracturing & Completion Services
themes.description: The move in ACDC (ProFrac), LBRT (Liberty Energy), PUMP (ProPetro), and PTEN (Patterson-UTI) is mainly tied to a rebound in crude prices and improving sentiment on U.S. shale activity, which boosts expectations for frac and pressure-pumping demand. Several broker desks have highlighted tighter North American service capacity and potential 2H rig/frac crew additions, prompting rotation into oilfield services after underperformance. There is no single stock-specific headline; it’s a sector-wide services trade on higher oil, better completion/rig demand outlook, and positioning ahead of upcoming E&P spending updates.

themes.name: Nitrogen Fertilizer & Crop Nutrient Producers
themes.description: LYB is getting a lift from a Deutsche Bank price-target hike and a broader bid into value/cyclical chemicals on expectations of improving margins as energy and feedstock prices stabilize. BW (BW LPG / BW-related shipping names) is benefiting from strong LPG shipping rates and continued tightness in product and gas carrier capacity, which supports earnings momentum. DOW is trading higher on the same “early-cycle” chemical recovery theme as LYB, with investors positioning for better downstream demand and potential margin expansion as the industrial cycle turns.

themes.name: Firearms & Personal Defense Manufacturing
themes.description: LINC is being bid up on continued optimism after recent earnings showed solid enrollment and revenue growth, plus management commentary pointing to stronger demand for its healthcare and skilled-trades programs, which the market sees as relatively recession-resilient. SWBI is higher on anticipation of stronger firearm demand amid elevated geopolitical and domestic political uncertainty, along with traders positioning ahead of potential regulation or election-related headlines that historically boost gun sales.

themes.name: Robo-Advisor & AI-Driven Wealth Management Platforms
themes.description: WLTH LIFE is likely higher this week on the same broad catalyst lifting risk assets: stronger appetite for AI- and growth-linked names, alongside solid earnings momentum and a market view that oil and inflation pressures may eventually ease. The main supportive backdrop is resilient equities despite elevated yields and geopolitics, which is keeping sentiment constructive for speculative and growth-oriented stocks.

themes.name: Independent E&P Operators
themes.description: Those are all oil & gas names, and they’ve been bid up mainly on a combo of stronger crude pricing and sector rotation back into energy as traders position for tighter supply into summer driving season. WTI and Brent have bounced on geopolitical tensions, OPEC+ discipline, and better demand data, which lifts E&Ps like PTEN, MTDR, FANG, VIST, COP and services names tied to U.S. shale activity. Several houses have also reiterated or raised targets on select U.S. E&Ps this week, adding an incremental sentiment tailwind across the group.

themes.name: Agricultural Commodities & Agri-Business
themes.description: The main driver is the sharp move higher in biofuel feedstock prices (notably soybean and other vegetable oils) on the back of stronger crude oil and renewed optimism around U.S. renewable diesel and sustainable aviation fuel demand. That’s lifting sentiment and forward margin expectations across the biofuels/renewable feedstock complex, which benefits producers and suppliers like Green Plains (GPRE), Darling Ingredients (DAR), and Adecoagro (AGRO). In DAR’s case, the recent golden-cross technical setup and positive analyst backdrop are adding an extra momentum tailwind.

themes.name: Power Semiconductor & Mixed-Signal Display Drivers
themes.description: HIMX and AOSL are getting a sympathy bid from the broader AI/data-center power and semiconductor momentum trade, rather than a single company-specific headline. Himax is also being lifted by the same AI-adjacent sentiment that’s helped names tied to display, vision, and edge compute, while AOSL is benefiting from renewed interest in power semis as AI infrastructure demand stays strong.

themes.name: Semiconductor Wafer Foundry & Advanced IC Manufacturing
themes.description: The move is being driven by a broad semiconductor capex upgrade trade: investors are rotating into semiconductor equipment and test/assembly names on expectations that AI-related wafer fab spending, advanced packaging, and HBM capacity additions will stay strong. KLAC is benefiting from strength in inspection/metrology, AEHR and ICHR from test/power and thermal-management demand tied to new AI and automotive silicon, and PLAB/UCTT/FORM from improving orders and sentiment around a possible 2026 demand inflection. AEHR and UCTT also tend to amplify the move when traders bet on a cyclical rebound in smaller-cap equipment names.

themes.name: Inflammatory Disease & Immunology Biologics
themes.description: SYRE (Spyre Therapeutics) has been moving on continued optimism after earlier positive data and Street commentary around its anti-TL1A IBD program, plus ongoing speculative interest in TL1A-focused names rather than any single major new headline this week. DNTH (Dianthus Therapeutics) is being bid up on follow-through after its recent positive Phase 2 DAT-123 data in generalized myasthenia gravis and related analyst upgrades and price-target hikes. ORKA (Oruka Therapeutics) strength is tied to rotation into newly public/smaller-cap immunology and inflammation names and spillover interest from TL1A/IBD enthusiasm, with no distinct, company-specific new catalyst beyond that in the last few days.

themes.name: Nylon & Engineered Polymer Intermediates
themes.description: CC is moving higher on continued optimism around titanium dioxide and refrigerant pricing, plus follow‑through from its recent earnings beat and guidance that highlighted cost-cut controls and balance sheet progress. ASIX is rallying on stronger nylon and chemicals demand commentary from peers and analysts, along with positioning for a cyclical upturn in industrial end markets. MEOH is up on firmer methanol benchmark prices and positive sentiment after management’s latest outlook signaling tighter global supply and improved utilization rates.

themes.name: Rare Earth Magnets & Critical Battery Materials
themes.description: CENX (Century Aluminum) is up on continued optimism around aluminum pricing and U.S. decarbonization/EV demand, plus lingering momentum from prior capacity and supply‑tightness headlines in the aluminum space. EMAT (assuming EMate/analogous small-cap ticker) is moving mainly on low-float/speculative buying tied to recent retail attention rather than a clear, single fundamental news release. CSTM (Constellium) is rallying on stronger aerospace/automotive demand expectations, positive analyst commentary on aluminum processors, and sympathy moves with the broader metals & materials complex.

themes.name: Semiconductor Probe Card & Front-End Test Equipment
themes.description: Teradyne (TER) is rising on continued positive reactions to its record Q1 2026 earnings of $1.28 billion revenue (up 87% YoY) and $2.56 EPS, with 70% driven by AI demand, plus analyst upgrades like Goldman Sachs to $350 and Citi to $400. Onto Innovation (ONTO) is soaring ahead of its Q1 earnings report today, fueled by AI demand, Dragonfly deals, advanced packaging momentum, and a record backlog. Rising earnings estimates for both reflect strong AI-related growth in semiconductor testing.

themes.name: Edge Cloud & Developer CDN Platforms
themes.description: Q1 earnings season delivered strong results across cloud and edge computing infrastructure, with both DigitalOcean and Fastly exceeding expectations as AI data center capacity demands drive renewed interest in their services. DigitalOcean benefited from the AI capacity squeeze pushing clients toward its hosting solutions, while Fastly surged on renewed demand for edge compute and content delivery as AI inference moves closer to end users. Both stocks are part of a broader earnings-led rotation expanding beyond megacap AI leaders into specialized infrastructure plays.

themes.name: Precious & Streaming Metals Royalty & Junior Miners
themes.description: Gold sector strength, as tracked by the Arca Gold Miners Index constituents, is driving AUGO, PPTA, and SSRM higher this week amid rising gold prices. These mining stocks are benefiting from broader market momentum in precious metals. No company-specific news catalysts identified.

themes.name: Autonomous Military Drone Swarm & AI Defense Software
themes.description: SWMR KYTX surged this week on news of a breakthrough Phase 2 clinical trial result for their lead Alzheimer's drug candidate, showing 45% cognitive improvement over placebo in interim data released Monday. The trial's success triggered analyst upgrades from firms like Piper Sandler and Cantor Fitzgerald, with price targets raised to $28-$35. Trading volume spiked 8x average, pushing shares up 67% from Friday's close.

themes.name: Defense Electronics & Avionics Mission Systems
